assertions.description: Describes the KYC-SA portal on swift.com/myswift as the submission mechanism
assertions.rationale: Response explicitly states 'The KYC-SA attestation is submitted through the SWIFT MySwift portal at swift.com/myswift' in Step 3.1.

assertions.description: Explains that an independent assessor is required and lists who qualifies: internal audit (if independent from SWIFT ops), external auditor with SWIFT CSP competency, or SWIFT-certified assessors
assertions.rationale: Response has a full Step 2 on independent assessor requirements. It lists all three types: Internal Audit (must be independent from SWIFT operations), External Auditor (qualified with SWIFT CSP competency), and SWIFT-Certified Assessors (SWIFT's formal credentials). The independence requirement is clearly stated.

assertions.description: States the v2026 attestation window (July 1 – December 31, 2026) and consequences of late submission including counterparty notifications
assertions.rationale: Response states the window in Step 4: 'July 1, 2026' opening and 'December 31, 2026' deadline. Consequences listed include: 'SWIFT notifies your counterparties that you have not attested' and 'potential suspension from SWIFT services.'

assertions.description: Explains the three attestation statuses for each control: Implemented, Partially Implemented, Not Implemented
assertions.rationale: Response explicitly lists all three statuses in a table in Step 3.2 with definitions for each: Implemented, Partially Implemented, and Not Implemented.

assertions.description: Notes that submitted attestations are visible to counterparties who can view compliance status and may request clarification
assertions.rationale: Response in Step 5 states 'Your attestation is immediately visible to your counterparties once submitted' and describes what counterparties see including per-control status. It also notes 'Counterparties may contact you to request clarification on controls marked Partially Implemented.'
